description: How human societies have developed, what events shaped them, and what patterns emerge across time.

sectors.name: Ancient History
sectors.description: Civilizations before 500 CE — Mesopotamia, Greece, Rome, China, India, Egypt

sectors.name: Medieval History
sectors.description: 500-1500 CE — feudalism, Islamic golden age, Byzantine empire, Mongol expansion

sectors.name: Contemporary History
sectors.description: 1900-present — world wars, cold war, decolonization, globalization

sectors.name: Regional History
sectors.description: History of specific regions — Africa, Asia, Americas, Middle East, Oceania

sectors.name: Historiography
sectors.description: How history is studied, major schools of historical thought, sources and methods

exceptions.ANC: Primary sources and classical texts acceptable regardless of age; modern translations/analyses should be within 30 years
exceptions.MEH: Medieval primary sources acceptable; scholarly analysis within 25 years
exceptions.MOD: Primary sources from period acceptable; scholarly analysis within 20 years
exceptions.CON: Maximum 15 years for latest_data role; primary sources from period acceptable
exceptions.REG: Regional studies within 20 years; historical primary sources acceptable
exceptions.HIS: Historiographical works within 15 years to capture current methodological debates

cross_domain_relationships.shared_territory: 20th century international relations as historical record
World wars, cold war, conflict history
